archetype: Real-Time Dielectric Breakdown Probability & Partial Discharge Phase-Resolved Mapping Engine for High-Voltage SF6 Insulated Gas Circuit Breakers
description: This system ingests nanosecond-resolution current and voltage transients alongside acoustic emission data from live gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) to predict dielectric breakdown events before they occur. It is used by grid reliability engineers to correlate micro-arcing signatures with specific fault geometries, requiring the fusion of physics-based simulation with real-time signal processing. The technical difficulty lies in processing terabytes of high-frequency waveform data in real-time while adhering to IEC 60447 compliance for partial discharge classification.
rules: MUST strictly adhere to IEC 60270 and IEEE C37.04 standards for partial discharge characterization and high-voltage equipment testing.
MUST implement deterministic real-time processing with sub-microsecond latency to capture transient arcs that last only a few cycles of the 50/60Hz grid frequency.
MUST support Phase-Resolved Partial Discharge (PRPD) pattern analysis using IEC 62478 optical and acoustic sensor fusion protocols.
MUST ensure data integrity for regulatory reporting under NERC CIP cybersecurity frameworks for critical infrastructure protection.
MUST utilize hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) simulation capabilities to validate breakdown models against known fault signatures before deployment.
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely on post-event batch processing, as dielectric breakdown can cause catastrophic equipment explosion within milliseconds of initial arcing.

DO NOT use generic anomaly detection models without domain-specific feature engineering for PRPD 'fingerprints,' as noise from corona discharge and external interference creates high false-positive rates.
DO NOT decouple the signal acquisition layer from the analysis engine, as synchronization drift between voltage phase angles and acoustic sensors will render the phase-resolved mapping invalid.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronizing heterogeneous data streams (UHF electromagnetic sensors, acoustic emission transducers, and optical current transformers) with nanosecond precision across Ethernet networks.
Performing real-time Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and Wavelet Decomposition on 100MHz+ sampling rates without dropping packets or introducing jitter.
Modeling the non-linear, stochastic physics of SF6 gas ionization and arc propagation in varying thermal and pressure conditions using embedded approximations.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Guarantees memory safety and deterministic real-time execution required for high-voltage protection logic.
recommended_stack.framework: Tokio with custom real-time kernel patches — Provides asynchronous I/O with sub-millisecond latency guarantees for sensor polling.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB with compressed hypertables — Optimized for high-velocity time-series ingestion and rapid querying of historical discharge patterns.
recommended_stack.cache: RocksDB (embedded) — Provides low-latency, persistent key-value storage for stateful signal buffering on edge nodes.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ (ZMQ) — Offers deterministic, lock-free messaging for streaming high-bandwidth waveform data between acquisition and analysis nodes.
special_services: Real-time PRPD Pattern Classifier (PyTorch ONNX Runtime)
SF6 Gas Dielectric Strength Physics Simulator
